concerns.text: Research question claims to evaluate 'statistical validity' but methodology only tests internal consistency. While acknowledged in limitations, this is a fundamental construct validity mismatch that affects whether the project can answer its stated research question without raw data access.

Fixed 0.05 absolute p-value difference threshold is statistically questionable across different p-value magnitudes. A relative or log-scale threshold would be more methodologically sound for comparing p-values at different scales (e.g., p=0.01 vs p=0.50).

Inconsistency detection logic is only validated on synthetic data. No validation exists on real A/B test summaries where ground truth inconsistency is known, limiting confidence in real-world detection performance.

The 0.05 baseline from John et al. (2022) concerns 'questionable research practices' not specifically A/B test reporting inconsistency. This may not be appropriate for the power analysis parameters detecting inconsistency proportion.
